name: Connector Sync State
description: A generic per-connector bookkeeping table (public.connector_sync_state) plus three RPCs (connector_sync_begin, connector_sync_success, connector_sync_error) that let any capture connector remember where it left off and whether its last run succeeded. Each connector keeps one row per (connector, surface, sync_key) holding a resume cursor, a high-watermark, last-started/finished/success/error timestamps, the last error, and a running error count. Keyed by connector identity — it stores no raw source text and references no thoughts, so the canonical UUID thoughts.id never appears. Pairs with brain-health-monitoring to alert on stalled or erroring connectors.
